articles.title: Anthropic Dreaming — AI That Writes Notes to Its Future Self
articles.summary: Anthropic has shipped a capability where AI agents review their own past sessions and write plain-text notes to future versions of themselves, creating the first auditable AI self-correction loop. Braygent's take: this is architecture-level news.

articles.title: Google I/O Tomorrow — Gemini 3.5 at 1/20th the Cost of GPT-5.5
articles.summary: Gemini 3.5 Flash has leaked with performance at 92% of GPT-5.5 at 15 to 20 times lower cost. Braygent's take: if the numbers hold, the cost of frontier-class AI just dropped by a factor of 20 for a massive category of workloads.

articles.title: Agent Hack SF — 24-Hour Hackathon with Anthropic, OpenAI, and Cohere APIs — May 24
articles.summary: Agent Hack SF on May 24 is a free 24-hour hackathon with 200 builders, API access to Anthropic, OpenAI, and Cohere, and prizes across three tracks including most useful agent and best tool calling. Past winners have gone on to raise funding.

articles.title: AI in Production Mini Conference — San Francisco — May 22
articles.summary: AI in Production Mini Conference in San Francisco on May 22 brings 104 practitioners together to discuss deploying and scaling AI in real production environments. The event focuses on practical challenges: inference cost, reliability, monitoring, and pipeline robustness.
